title: Mineralization of Nutrients During Biological Degradation of Wheat Straw
abstract:
Mineralizing activities cf different inoculated microorganisms during degradation of straw
were studied. The inoculated microorganisms showed an increased release of ammonical and
nitrate nitrogen during degradation. Inoculation has proved its special role in mineralization
of phosphorus. Penicilliltm sp. was found to be most active in releasing available phosphorus.
The amount of potash declined as the degradation progressed.
